heroHeadline: Rubio Draws IRGC Red Line on Iran Delegation — Taremi Visa in Doubt; Brazil Loses Estevao to Hamstring Tear; 47 Days to Kickoff
heroSubtitle: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio confirmed on April 24 that Iran's players may attend the World Cup — 'Nothing from the US has told them they can't come' — but drew a hard line on accompanying IRGC-linked personnel: 'They can't bring a bunch of IRGC terrorists into our country and pretend they are journalists and athletic trainers.' The statement raises specific concern about Iran captain Mehdi Taremi (Inter Milan), who served at an IRGC Navy base in Bushehr 2010–2012, and multiple other squad members who completed mandatory IRGC service. Iran Football Federation President Mehdi Taj confirmed replacement players have been identified for those denied visas. Separately, Chelsea confirmed Brazilian winger Estevao (18) is out for the rest of the club season with a Grade 4 hamstring tear sustained April 18 against Manchester United — ESPN sources described his World Cup participation as 'highly unlikely'; coach Carlo Ancelotti announces Brazil's squad May 18. Brazil is now potentially without both Rodrygo (torn ACL, out entirely) and Estevao. The FIFA Congress in Vancouver on April 30 — 5 days away — remains the formal decision point on Iran's overall participation.
footerNote: Data sourced from FIFA.com, UEFA.com, US Soccer, CONCACAF, ESPN, The Athletic, BBC Sport, AP, Reuters, NPR, Al Jazeera, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, American Immigration Council, Middle East Monitor, CBS Sports, Bloomberg, Fortune, UPI, CNN, CNBC, NBC Dallas-Fort Worth, Fox 5 New York, Inside World Football, CBC Sports, Johnson County Post, PR Newswire, Yahoo Sports, RTE Sport, TRT World, Time, Xinhua, The National, HuffPost, Washington Post
